question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted configuration with a large number of entries in the `scenarios` map (e.g., 1000 named scenarios) to bypass the only guard (non-empty check) in `validate()`, causing the monitor to submit transactions for all scenarios simultaneously and increasing network node CPU/memory consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Field: responseThreads] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseThreads` to a very high value combined with `receiptPercent=1.0` in each scenario to ensure every transaction triggers a `TransactionReceiptQuery` executed concurrently across all threads, multiplying the number of queries hitting network nodes and increasing their resource consumption by 30%+?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Field: nodeMaxBackoff] Can an unprivileged external user set `nodeMaxBackoff` to an extremely small duration (e.g., 1 nanosecond) since there is no `@DurationMin` constraint on this field, causing the SDK client to retry failed node connections with near-zero delay and sustaining elevated request rates on network nodes beyond 30% of normal baseline?
